summary: Fix mismatched braces in report dedupe so the package compiles, and only return exit code 2 when the top row's score actually clears the 0.8 duplicate threshold.
reason: The first attempt to add the score-gated exit code left an orphan return statement and an extra closing brace between the inner if-block and the function's final return nil, producing five Go syntax errors on the build. The orphan return also made the score-threshold check redundant by exiting 2 for any non-empty result set.
validated_outcome: go build ./... passes; report_dedupe.go compiles and the score >= 0.8 gate is the sole exit-2 trigger.
